name: Joe Rogan
lens: Comedian & long-form interviewer. Frequently skeptical of government narratives and institutional expertise. Massive reach across political lines.

name: Ben Shapiro
lens: Conservative commentator and Daily Wire founder. Argues from a constitutionalist and free-market perspective. Known for rapid-fire debate style.

name: Jordan B. Peterson
lens: Clinical psychologist turned cultural commentator. Frames issues through individual responsibility, free speech, and Western philosophical tradition.

name: Trevor Noah
lens: South African comedian and former Daily Show host. Views U.S. politics through an international lens. Focuses on irony and how policy looks from outside America.

name: Theo Von
lens: Comedian and podcaster from rural Louisiana. Brings a working-class, Southern perspective. Disarming humor on serious topics.

name: Full Send / NELK Boys
lens: Entertainment brand that interviews political figures. Appeals to young men. Blurs the line between comedy and political commentary.

name: Candace Owens
lens: Conservative commentator and media personality. Critiques progressive orthodoxy, often from a contrarian angle. Vocal on cultural and religious issues.

name: Phil McGraw (Dr. Phil)
lens: TV psychologist turned Merit Street Media host. Approaches politics through a behavioral and common-sense framework.

name: Tucker Carlson
lens: Former Fox News anchor, now independent. Populist conservative skeptical of foreign intervention and corporate influence.

name: The Young Turks
lens: Progressive news and commentary network. Anti-corporate, pro-labor. One of the original online political shows.

name: Steven Crowder
lens: Conservative comedian and 'Change My Mind' creator. Confrontational debate style. Challenges progressive positions through street interviews.

name: The Breakfast Club
lens: Morning radio/podcast with Charlamagne Tha God, DJ Envy, and Jess Hilarious. Centers Black American perspective on politics and culture.

name: Russell Brand
lens: British comedian turned anti-establishment commentator. Focuses on institutional corruption, censorship, and spiritual themes.

name: Patrick Bet-David
lens: Iranian-American entrepreneur and Valuetainment founder. Views politics through a business and immigrant-success lens.

name: Dan Bongino
lens: Former Secret Service agent and NYPD officer. Brings a law enforcement perspective to conservative commentary.

name: Lex Fridman
lens: MIT AI researcher and long-form interviewer. Approaches politics with academic curiosity. Hosts leaders from all sides.

name: Asmongold
lens: Gaming streamer who frequently covers politics and culture. Appeals to young men. Known for blunt, unfiltered reactions.

name: HasanAbi
lens: Turkish-American leftist streamer and TYT alum. Anti-imperialist, pro-labor. Reaches young progressives through Twitch and YouTube.

name: Megyn Kelly
lens: Former Fox News and NBC anchor. Centrist-to-conservative, focuses on media criticism and parenting/culture issues.

name: Jon Stewart
lens: The Daily Show host and veteran satirist. Uses comedy to cut through political spin. Trusted across generations.

name: John Oliver
lens: Last Week Tonight host. Deep-dive investigative comedy. Makes complex policy accessible and funny.

name: Philip DeFranco
lens: OG YouTube news creator. Centrist who covers stories from multiple angles. Trusted by younger audiences for fair coverage.

name: David Pakman
lens: Progressive political commentator. Data-driven, measured tone. Focuses on policy substance over culture war.

name: Brian Tyler Cohen
lens: Progressive YouTube commentator. Rapid-response to breaking political news. Strong Democratic perspective.

name: Pod Save America
lens: Former Obama staffers. Progressive but insider perspective. Understands how the political machine actually works.

name: Bill Maher
lens: Real Time host. Liberal who frequently criticizes the left. Anti-woke, pro-free speech. Unpredictable takes.

name: Destiny
lens: Political streamer and debater. Center-left but challenges all sides. Known for rigorous fact-checking in real time.

name: Breaking Points
lens: Krystal Ball (left) and Saagar Enjeti (right) co-host. Populist, anti-establishment from both sides. Independent media model.

name: Shannon Sharpe
lens: NFL Hall of Famer turned cultural commentator. Club Shay Shay host. Brings a Black, Southern, sports-world perspective to news and culture.

name: Stephen A. Smith
lens: ESPN star turned independent voice. Loud, opinionated, crosses sports and politics. Massive Black male audience.

name: Don Lemon
lens: Former CNN anchor, now independent. Progressive, focuses on race and justice. Building a new media brand on YouTube.

name: Andrew Schulz
lens: Comedian and Flagrant podcast host. Irreverent, crosses every line. Appeals to young men who distrust media from all sides.

name: Tim Pool
lens: Independent journalist turned right-populist commentator. Timcast reaches millions daily. Anti-establishment, skeptical of mainstream media.

name: Matt Walsh
lens: Daily Wire host. Socially conservative, culture war focused. 'What Is a Woman?' documentary maker. Blunt, provocative style.

name: Michael Knowles
lens: Daily Wire host. Catholic conservative. Culture and policy from a traditional values perspective.

name: Charlamagne Tha God
lens: Radio host, author, mental health advocate. Centers Black American perspective. Willing to challenge both parties. Huge influence on young Black voters.

name: Cenk Uygur
lens: TYT founder. Progressive populist. Anti-corporate, pro-worker. Ran for Congress. One of the original online political voices.

name: Chris Cuomo
lens: Former CNN anchor, now at NewsNation. Repositioned as independent-minded. Focuses on accountability journalism and interviews.

name: Bari Weiss
lens: The Free Press founder. Left the NYT over 'illiberalism.' Center-left turned heterodox. Free speech, anti-censorship, pro-debate.

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
lens: Congresswoman and progressive icon. Uses social media like a creator, not a politician. Massive reach with young progressives.

name: Vivek Ramaswamy
lens: Entrepreneur, former presidential candidate, DOGE co-lead. New right populist. Appeals to young conservatives and tech-adjacent audiences.

name: Dan Crenshaw
lens: Congressman, former Navy SEAL. Conservative but willing to break with MAGA. Strong social media presence, especially short-form video.

name: Hasan Minhaj
lens: Comedian and former Patriot Act host. Indian-American Muslim perspective. Makes policy funny and accessible for young audiences.

name: Tim Dillon
lens: Comedian and podcaster. Satirizes politics, media, and corporate America. Irreverent, hard to pin ideologically. Rogan-adjacent audience.

name: Kyle Kulinski
lens: Secular Talk host. Progressive populist, Justice Democrats co-founder. Anti-corporate, anti-war. Reaches young left.

name: Glenn Greenwald
lens: Journalist, Snowden story breaker. Former left, now anti-establishment independent. Focuses on civil liberties, surveillance, media criticism.

name: Benny Johnson
lens: Conservative content creator and Turning Point USA chief creative officer. Viral short-form political content. Pro-MAGA.

name: Ana Kasparian
lens: Former TYT co-host, now independent. Left-to-center shift. Vocal about leaving progressive orthodoxy. Free speech advocate.

name: Dave Rubin
lens: The Rubin Report host. Former progressive turned conservative/libertarian. Free speech, anti-woke. Long-form interviews.

name: Matt Taibbi
lens: Investigative journalist. Twitter Files reporter. Former Rolling Stone. Anti-censorship, skeptical of institutions from both left and right.

name: Jason Whitlock
lens: Sports journalist turned conservative cultural commentator. Fearless Podcast. Black conservative voice. Critiques BLM and progressive racial politics.

name: Clay Travis
lens: OutKick founder, sports and politics. Conservative populist. Merged sports fandom with right-leaning political commentary.

name: Tomi Lahren
lens: Conservative commentator. Short-form viral political content. Reaches young conservative women especially.

name: Tulsi Gabbard
lens: Former Democrat congresswoman turned independent/Republican. Anti-war, anti-establishment. National security perspective from a veteran.

name: Mark Levin
lens: Conservative radio host, Fox News host. Constitutional lawyer. 'The Great One.' Deep-cut conservative legal and policy arguments.

name: Elon Musk
lens: Owner of X/Twitter, Tesla, SpaceX CEO, DOGE head. The most followed person on the platform he owns. Every post moves markets and politics.

name: Logan Paul
lens: YouTuber, WWE wrestler, PRIME founder. Massive Gen Z/young millennial reach. When he weighs in on news, millions hear it.

name: Kai Cenat
lens: Twitch's biggest streamer. Massive Gen Z cultural influence, especially young Black audiences. When he talks news, it reaches people who never watch CNN.

name: Kara Swisher
lens: Tech journalist and podcast host. Covers the intersection of tech, power, and politics. Holds Silicon Valley accountable.

name: Ezra Klein
lens: NYT opinion columnist and podcaster. Policy wonk. Deep structural analysis of politics. Reaches educated progressives and centrists.

name: Jimmy Dore
lens: Comedian turned anti-establishment political commentator. Attacks Democrats from the left. Populist, anti-war, anti-corporate.

name: Jesse Watters
lens: Fox News primetime host. Populist conservative with a confrontational style. Huge reach with right-leaning audiences.

name: Rachel Maddow
lens: MSNBC host and podcast creator. Deep investigative progressive commentary. Reaches older and engaged Democratic base.

name: Sean Hannity
lens: Fox News anchor and radio host. Pro-Trump conservative. One of the most-watched voices on the American right.

name: Zuby
lens: British-Nigerian rapper and author. Common sense conservatism. Anti-woke, pro-personal responsibility. Popular with young right-leaning audiences globally.

name: ContraPoints
lens: Trans leftist YouTuber and video essayist. Philosophy-driven deep dives on culture, politics, gender. Influential with young progressives.

name: SNEAKO
lens: Controversial content creator. Manosphere-adjacent, reaches young men. Provocative takes on culture and politics.

name: Nikki Haley
lens: Former UN Ambassador and presidential candidate. Establishment Republican, hawkish on foreign policy. Represents anti-Trump wing of GOP.

name: Bernie Sanders
lens: Senator and two-time presidential candidate. Democratic socialist. Anti-billionaire, pro-worker. Massive young following despite his age.

name: JiDion
lens: Gen Z content creator and prankster. Crosses entertainment and cultural commentary. Reaches young men who don't consume traditional news.

name: Danny Gonzalez
lens: Commentary YouTuber. Critiques internet culture and media. Reaches young audiences who process news through creator commentary.

name: Drew Gooden
lens: Commentary YouTuber. Deadpan media criticism and cultural analysis. Gen Z favorite for making sense of online chaos.

name: Val Demings
lens: Former congresswoman and police chief. Black woman moderate Democrat. Law enforcement perspective from the left.

name: Laura Ingraham
lens: Fox News host. Conservative populist. Anti-immigration, pro-Trump. One of the most-watched women in conservative media.

name: Hbomberguy
lens: British leftist video essayist. Deep investigative content on media, plagiarism, and politics. Influential with young progressives.

name: Briahna Joy Gray
lens: Former Bernie Sanders press secretary. Progressive critic of Democratic establishment. Bad Faith podcast host.

name: Meghan McCain
lens: Former View co-host. Republican but anti-Trump dynasty. Represents traditional GOP perspective, especially on military/foreign policy.

name: Sage Steele
lens: Former ESPN anchor gone independent. Biracial woman, moderate-conservative. Critiques media groupthink. Sports-to-politics crossover.

name: All-In Podcast
lens: Four tech billionaires (Chamath, Calacanis, Sacks, Friedberg) debate politics, economics, and tech from a Silicon Valley insider perspective.

name: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
lens: Former presidential candidate turned HHS Secretary known for vaccine skepticism and anti-establishment populism that crosses traditional party lines.

name: MeidasTouch
lens: Progressive political media network producing rapid-response anti-MAGA content and investigative reporting.

name: Mehdi Hasan
lens: British-American journalist and Zeteo founder known for aggressive interviewing style and progressive foreign policy criticism.

name: Nate Silver
lens: Data journalist and election forecaster; now writes Silver Bulletin with a contrarian, probabilistic worldview.

name: Matt Yglesias
lens: Liberal policy wonk and Slow Boring author who champions YIMBY housing, immigration, and economic growth from a center-left perspective.

name: Van Jones
lens: CNN political commentator and criminal justice reform advocate who bridges progressive activism with bipartisan coalition-building.

name: Joy Reid
lens: Former MSNBC host offering sharp progressive commentary centered on race, democracy, and media accountability.

name: Jake Tapper
lens: CNN anchor known for holding politicians accountable across party lines with a fact-first approach.

name: Ron DeSantis
lens: Florida Governor and former presidential candidate who champions culture-war conservatism and anti-woke policies.

name: Gavin Newsom
lens: California Governor and prominent national Democrat who uses social media to counter Republican narratives and champion progressive policy.

name: Ted Cruz
lens: Texas Senator and Verdict podcast host who frames issues through constitutional originalism and combative conservative populism.

name: Elizabeth Warren
lens: Massachusetts Senator who champions consumer protection, anti-monopoly policy, and Wall Street accountability.

name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
lens: Firebrand Georgia congresswoman and MAGA loyalist known for provocative statements and hard-right populist positions.

name: JD Vance
lens: Vice President and Hillbilly Elegy author representing the new populist right's blend of economic nationalism and social conservatism.

name: Roland Martin
lens: Veteran journalist and #RolandMartinUnfiltered host covering news through a Black American lens with unapologetic progressive commentary.

name: Ana Navarro
lens: Republican strategist turned anti-Trump CNN/ABC commentator who critiques the GOP from a center-right, pro-immigration perspective.

name: Jorge Ramos
lens: Legendary Univision anchor who covers U.S. politics through a Latino and immigrant rights lens.

name: Chris Williamson
lens: Modern Wisdom podcast host exploring psychology, philosophy, and culture through long-form conversations with a male self-improvement lens.

name: Shawn Ryan Show
lens: Former Navy SEAL hosting long-form interviews on military, intelligence, and national security with a patriotic, veteran-centered perspective.

name: MrBeast
lens: Most-subscribed YouTuber whose massive platform occasionally intersects with politics, philanthropy, and cultural influence.

name: Ethan Klein
lens: H3 Podcast host mixing comedy with progressive political takes and internet culture commentary.

name: Lincoln Project
lens: Anti-Trump Republican PAC producing viral political ads aimed at defeating MAGA candidates from within the conservative tradition.

name: Killer Mike
lens: Grammy-winning rapper and Atlanta activist blending hip-hop culture with progressive economic and racial justice advocacy.

name: Adam Schiff
lens: California Senator and former House Intelligence Committee chair; leading Democratic voice on Trump accountability and rule of law.

name: Pete Buttigieg
lens: Former Transportation Secretary known for articulate, rhetorically sharp center-left messaging on social media.

name: Noah Smith
lens: Economist and Noahpinion author writing about industrial policy, immigration, and geopolitics from a pragmatic center-left perspective.

name: Scott Galloway
lens: NYU professor and Prof G/Pivot podcast host critiquing Big Tech monopolies and inequality with irreverent, data-heavy commentary.

name: Fareed Zakaria
lens: CNN host and Washington Post columnist analyzing global affairs from a centrist, internationalist perspective.

name: Krystal Ball
lens: Breaking Points co-host delivering populist progressive analysis critical of both parties' establishment wings.

name: Chris Hedges
lens: Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist offering radical left critiques of corporate power, militarism, and American empire.

name: Cornel West
lens: Philosopher, activist, and former presidential candidate speaking on racial justice and democratic socialism from a prophetic moral tradition.

name: Bret Baier
lens: Fox News chief political anchor representing the network's straight-news wing with a center-right editorial lens.

name: Martha MacCallum
lens: Fox News anchor covering politics from a traditional center-right perspective with a focus on historical context.

name: Judd Legum
lens: Investigative journalist who tracks corporate accountability, dark money in politics, and the gap between what companies say publicly and what they fund privately. Founded Popular Information to follow the money behind political influence campaigns.

name: David Sirota
lens: Investigative journalist and founder of The Lever, focused on how corporate power and money in politics shape policy outcomes. Former Bernie Sanders speechwriter who applies an insider's understanding of political machinery to accountability reporting.

name: Isaac Saul
lens: Founder of Tangle, a nonpartisan daily newsletter that presents the strongest arguments from left, right, and center on the day's top political story. Focuses on steelmanning opposing views rather than advocating for a particular side.

name: Ryan Grim
lens: Co-founder of Drop Site News and former Washington bureau chief of The Intercept. Investigative reporter covering progressive politics, Capitol Hill power dynamics, and the internal battles shaping the Democratic Party.

name: Jeremy Scahill
lens: Co-founder of Drop Site News and author of Blackwater and Dirty Wars. One of the foremost reporters on U.S. covert operations, drone warfare, and the national security state. Brings deep sourcing in military and intelligence communities.

name: Jennifer Rubin
lens: Former Washington Post columnist who left corporate media to co-found The Contrarian with Norm Eisen. Evolved from conservative to democracy-focused commentator analyzing threats to rule of law, judicial independence, and democratic institutions.

name: Adam Tooze
lens: Columbia University historian and author of Crashed and Shutdown. Writes Chartbook, connecting global economic data, geopolitical shifts, and historical patterns to explain how financial systems and power structures actually work.

name: Robert Reich
lens: Former U.S. Secretary of Labor and UC Berkeley professor. Analyzes economic inequality, labor rights, and corporate concentration of power through the lens of decades of policymaking experience. Over one million Substack subscribers.

name: Paul Krugman
lens: Nobel Prize-winning economist and former New York Times columnist. Brings rigorous macroeconomic analysis to fiscal policy, trade, inequality, and the political economy of misinformation. Now writes independently on Substack after leaving the Times.

name: Radley Balko
lens: Investigative journalist and author of Rise of the Warrior Cop. Writes The Watch, focused on police accountability, criminal justice reform, forensic science failures, and civil liberties. Uses deep case reporting to expose systemic problems in law enforcement.

name: Joyce Vance
lens: Former U.S. Attorney for the Northern District of Alabama and MSNBC legal analyst. Writes Civil Discourse, translating complex federal legal proceedings, DOJ operations, and constitutional questions into accessible analysis grounded in prosecutorial experience.

name: Emily Atkin
lens: Founder of HEATED, an accountability-focused climate newsletter. Tracks who is responsible for the climate crisis and who is blocking action, with a focus on fossil fuel industry influence, greenwashing, and the politics of climate policy.

name: Spencer Ackerman
lens: National security journalist and author of Reign of Terror, which traced the through-line from the War on Terror to domestic authoritarianism. Writes Forever Wars, analyzing how U.S. security policy, surveillance, and militarism shape politics at home and abroad.

name: Pablo Manriquez
lens: Editor of Migrant Insider, a daily immigration newsletter covering ICE, Border Patrol, USCIS, deportations, and immigration court from Capitol Hill. Brings bilingual, on-the-ground reporting that connects policy decisions to real enforcement outcomes.

name: Austin Kocher
lens: Syracuse University researcher and founder of the Deportation Data Project. Uses FOIA requests and data analysis to make immigration enforcement transparent, tracking ICE detention numbers, deportation trends, and the gap between government claims and empirical reality.

name: Matt Pearce
lens: Former LA Times reporter and president of Media Guild of the West. Writes about the media industry's structural collapse, newsroom labor fights, and how the business model crisis in journalism affects what stories get told and which don't.

name: Yascha Mounk
lens: Johns Hopkins political scientist and founder of Persuasion. Studies democratic backsliding, identity politics, and liberal institutional resilience. Author of The Identity Trap, arguing that a new ideology threatens the foundations of pluralist democracy.

name: Ruy Teixeira
lens: AEI senior fellow and co-author of Where Have All the Democrats Gone. Writes The Liberal Patriot, analyzing Democratic coalition dynamics, working-class voter realignment, and why center-left parties keep losing ground on cultural issues.

name: Tom Bonier
lens: CEO of The TARA Group and former TargetSmart senior advisor. Writes The Data Dive, analyzing voter registration trends, early vote patterns, and demographic shifts using granular data rather than punditry. Known for spotting the post-Dobbs registration surge early.

name: Merrill Goozner
lens: Veteran healthcare journalist and former editor-in-chief of Modern Healthcare. Writes GoozNews, providing informed commentary on drug pricing, insurance markets, public health policy, and the structural forces driving U.S. healthcare costs.

name: Under the Desk News (V Spehar)
lens: TikTok journalist who delivers daily news briefings from under a desk, breaking complex current events into digestible 60-second segments. Brings an accessible, LGBTQ+-affirming perspective that centers social justice and representation in news coverage.

name: Jessica Yellin
lens: Former CNN Chief White House Correspondent who founded News Not Noise to deliver clear, non-partisan news explanation. Focuses on cutting through media noise and political spin to help audiences understand what actually matters in policy and politics.

name: Lisa Remillard
lens: Former TV news anchor who transitioned to TikTok as 'The News Girl,' delivering daily national news in 60-second fast-fact segments. Brings a traditional broadcast journalism lens to short-form social media, prioritizing factual reporting over opinion.

name: Ari Melber
lens: MSNBC host and licensed attorney who blends legal analysis, political reporting, and cultural commentary. Brings a constitutional law perspective to breaking political news, frequently connecting hip-hop culture and legal precedent in his analysis.

name: Jessica Valenti
lens: Feminist columnist and author who reports daily on abortion rights in the United States through her Substack 'Abortion, Every Day.' Brings a reproductive rights and gender equity lens to policy, tracking every legal and legislative development affecting bodily autonomy.

name: The Law Says What
lens: Harvard-trained attorneys Maclen and Ashleigh Stanley who break down legal news and court cases for a general audience. Bring a non-partisan legal education lens, making constitutional law, Supreme Court rulings, and everyday legal concepts accessible.

name: Gregory Price
lens: Conservative political commentator and former Deputy Rapid Response Director for Trump's 2024 campaign. Brings an insider's perspective on Republican strategy and messaging, framing news through a pro-Trump, rapid-response political lens.

name: Isabel Brown
lens: Gen Z conservative commentator, author, and Turning Point USA contributor who breaks down politics and culture for a young audience. Frames issues through a traditionalist, free-market perspective with a focus on reaching younger voters.

name: Bogdan Laurentiu
lens: Romanian immigrant and conservative content creator who founded Immigrants for Liberty. Brings a pro-legal-immigration, pro-America perspective that challenges the assumption that immigrant communities lean left, advocating for assimilation and lawful pathways.

name: Joey Contino
lens: Executive audio producer at iHeartMedia who covers global events on TikTok, with deep expertise in the war in Ukraine and international conflicts. Prioritizes factual, neutral reporting while bringing a unique international affairs lens and documentary-making background.

name: So Informed (Jess Natale)
lens: Brooklyn-based solo political explainer who delivers infographic-driven breakdowns of politics and social issues across six platforms. Brings a progressive, policy-focused lens born out of grassroots campaign work, emphasizing accessible civic education.

name: Stacey Abrams
lens: Politician, lawyer, and voting rights activist who founded Fair Fight Action and is widely credited with expanding voter turnout in Georgia. Brings a voting rights, democratic access, and racial equity lens to every political issue, drawing on direct legislative experience.

name: Motaz Azaiza
lens: Palestinian photojournalist from Gaza whose powerful images of the conflict brought human suffering under siege to global attention. Brings a firsthand, on-the-ground perspective from a conflict zone where foreign press access is restricted, filling a critical media void.

name: Taylor Lorenz
lens: Internet culture journalist and former Washington Post reporter who launched User Mag on Substack. Brings a tech-and-creator-economy lens to politics, tracking how platforms, algorithms, and online influence shape public discourse and power.

name: Jonah Goldberg
lens: Editor-in-chief and co-founder of The Dispatch, a center-right newsmagazine. A conservative who resigned from Fox News over concerns about misinformation, bringing a traditional-conservative, institutionalist lens that frequently critiques populism from the right.

name: Jim Sciutto
lens: CNN Chief National Security Analyst and former ABC News correspondent with deep expertise in intelligence, defense, and foreign policy. Brings a national security lens shaped by years covering China, the Middle East, and U.S. intelligence operations.

name: Joe Concha
lens: Conservative media critic, political columnist, and Fox News contributor. Brings a right-leaning media criticism lens, analyzing how mainstream outlets cover politics and challenging what he sees as liberal bias in news coverage.

name: Jill Filipovic
lens: Attorney, freelance journalist, and author who writes on gender equity, reproductive rights, and law. Brings a feminist legal lens to politics, combining her legal training with international reporting experience to analyze how policy affects women's rights.

name: Alaina Wood
lens: Award-winning climate communicator and sustainability scientist who fights climate doomism with her 'Good Climate News' series. Brings a solutions-oriented environmental lens, highlighting corporate accountability while countering eco-anxiety with evidence-based optimism.

name: Hazel Thayer
lens: Canadian-based climate and politics creator with an economics degree who uses biting humor to simplify sustainability and environmental economics. Brings a climate-economics lens, connecting fossil fuel policy, greenwashing, and political decisions to their environmental consequences.

name: Chuck Schumer
lens: Senate Democratic Leader from New York. Frames issues through pragmatic coalition-building and New York constituency interests. Key voice on spending, immigration, and judicial confirmations.

name: Mitch McConnell
lens: Longtime Senate Republican leader from Kentucky and institutional strategist. Approaches politics through procedural mastery and judicial appointments. Often at odds with populist wing of his own party.

name: John Fetterman
lens: Pennsylvania senator known for breaking with progressive orthodoxy on issues like Israel and immigration. Blue-collar populist style with an irreverent social media presence that defies easy categorization.

name: Tim Scott
lens: Republican senator from South Carolina and former presidential candidate. Frames conservative policies through opportunity and optimism. Notable voice on race, economic mobility, and faith.

name: Mitt Romney
lens: Former presidential nominee and Utah senator known for breaking with his party on Trump-era issues. Represents traditional Republican values of fiscal conservatism and institutional norms.

name: Cory Booker
lens: New Jersey senator known for impassioned rhetoric on justice, community, and moral leadership. Frames policy through empathy, civil rights, and grassroots organizing.

name: Lindsey Graham
lens: South Carolina senator and senior Judiciary Committee member. Hawkish on foreign policy, particularly on Iran and defense. Known for shifting alliances between establishment and Trump-aligned positions.

name: Josh Hawley
lens: Missouri senator and prominent voice in the populist-conservative movement. Champions anti-Big Tech regulation, economic nationalism, and working-class conservatism. Frequent critic of corporate America.

name: Katie Britt
lens: Alabama's youngest-ever female senator. Rising Republican voice on family policy, immigration enforcement, and economic issues. Gained national attention for her State of the Union response.

name: Mark Kelly
lens: Arizona senator, Navy combat veteran, and retired NASA astronaut. Brings a national security and pragmatic centrist lens. Leading voice on gun safety reform after the shooting of his wife Gabby Giffords.

name: Raphael Warnock
lens: Georgia senator and senior pastor at Ebenezer Baptist Church. Frames policy through moral and faith-based arguments, particularly on voting rights, healthcare, and economic justice.

name: Chris Murphy
lens: Connecticut senator and the Senate's most vocal advocate for gun control since Sandy Hook. Also a leading voice on foreign policy restraint and rebuilding the progressive movement.

name: Marco Rubio
lens: 72nd U.S. Secretary of State and former Florida senator. Hawkish on China, Cuba, and Venezuela. Brings a foreign policy and national security lens as the top diplomat in the Trump administration.

name: Tom Cotton
lens: Arkansas senator and Army veteran. Hardline conservative on immigration, national defense, and China policy. Known for aggressive stances on law enforcement and military intervention.

name: Tammy Duckworth
lens: Illinois senator, Purple Heart veteran, and double amputee who lost her legs in Iraq. Advocates for veterans, disability rights, and military readiness. First Thai-American senator.

name: Hakeem Jeffries
lens: House Democratic Leader from Brooklyn. First Black leader of a major U.S. congressional party. Frames issues through institutional opposition to Republican agenda and coalition management.

name: Mike Johnson
lens: Speaker of the House from Louisiana. Constitutional lawyer by background. Frames policy through evangelical Christianity and strict constitutional interpretation.

name: Jim Jordan
lens: Ohio representative and House Judiciary Committee chair. Leading Republican voice on government oversight, weaponization of federal agencies, and free speech. Combative hearing style.

name: Jamie Raskin
lens: Maryland representative and constitutional law professor. Led Trump's second impeachment. Known for impassioned constitutional arguments and defense of democratic institutions.

name: Ilhan Omar
lens: Minnesota representative and one of the first Muslim women in Congress. Somali refugee who brings an immigration, anti-war, and progressive foreign policy perspective.

name: Ro Khanna
lens: Silicon Valley representative who bridges progressive politics and the tech industry. Advocates for industrial policy, tech regulation, and bringing manufacturing back to America.

name: Matt Gaetz
lens: Former Florida representative and Trump loyalist. Provocative MAGA voice who challenged Republican leadership. Nominated for Attorney General before withdrawing.

name: Byron Donalds
lens: Florida representative and rising Black conservative star. Challenges conventional identity politics narratives. Vocal on fiscal conservatism, deregulation, and school choice.

name: Jasmine Crockett
lens: Texas representative and civil rights attorney. Gained viral fame for sharp exchanges in committee hearings. Voices on racial justice, voting rights, and holding Republicans accountable.

name: Nancy Mace
lens: South Carolina representative and first woman to graduate from The Citadel. Positions herself as a maverick Republican, vocal on transgender issues and government spending. Unpredictable voting record.

name: Chip Roy
lens: Texas representative and Freedom Caucus member. Staunch fiscal conservative and border hawk who has clashed with his own party's leadership on spending bills.

name: Dean Withers
lens: Liberal TikTok debater from Colorado who shifted from conservative to progressive after January 6th. Known for confrontational political debates and live streams that draw millions of young viewers.

name: Damon Imani
lens: Iran-born, Denmark-based satirical commentator and content creator. Right-wing provocateur who uses humor and viral clips to critique mainstream media and progressive policies.

name: Matteo il Pazzeo
lens: Italian-American constitutional law explainer who breaks down Supreme Court rulings and executive actions in rapid-fire TikToks. Self-described constitutional expert with a left-leaning perspective.

name: Claudio Vallejo
lens: Texas-based progressive fact-checker and political commentator originally from California. Uses his platform for media literacy and has filed voting rights lawsuits. Self-described liberal.

name: Endeavorance
lens: Creator who produces current events skits and runs the 'Stuff Keeps Happening' newsletter and podcast. Uses humor and satire to cover politics and tech from a left-leaning perspective.

name: Kenneth Walden (2RawTooReal)
lens: Satirical political commentator credentialed at the 2024 DNC. Known for sharp takes on politics, law, race, and culture from a progressive perspective.

name: Andra Berghoff
lens: Minnesota-based news and pop culture commentator with a digital design background. Covers politics, culture, and race from a self-described left-leaning perspective.

name: Catherine Cannon
lens: Florida-based educator and politics explainer with 15+ years teaching experience. Self-described leftist who produces accessible political commentary and hosts the 'Just Buy Less Coffee' podcast.

name: Kevin Roberts (Heritage Foundation)
lens: President of the Heritage Foundation and architect of Project 2025. Leading voice in the conservative policy establishment pushing for sweeping government restructuring.

name: Brookings Institution
lens: Centrist-to-center-left think tank producing independent research on governance, economics, and foreign policy. One of the most cited policy research organizations in Washington.

name: Cato Institute
lens: Libertarian think tank promoting individual liberty, free markets, and limited government. Critiques both progressive overreach and conservative authoritarianism.

name: Center for American Progress
lens: Progressive think tank closely aligned with the Democratic establishment. Produces policy research on healthcare, climate, economy, and social justice that often shapes Democratic legislative priorities.

name: Manhattan Institute
lens: Conservative/free-market think tank focused on urban policy, education, energy, and economic opportunity. Influential on policing, housing, and anti-DEI policy debates.

name: American Enterprise Institute
lens: Center-right think tank focused on government, economics, and foreign/defense policy. Home to prominent neoconservative and center-right scholars.

name: Christiane Amanpour
lens: CNN's chief international anchor and the most recognized name in international journalism. Brings decades of war reporting and a global, human-rights-oriented perspective to U.S. foreign policy debates.

name: Richard Engel
lens: NBC News chief foreign correspondent who has covered every major conflict since the Iraq War. Provides ground-level reporting from war zones and authoritarian states that adds depth to foreign policy debates.

name: Bret Stephens
lens: New York Times conservative columnist and former Wall Street Journal editor. Neoconservative on foreign policy, center-right on domestic issues. Frequent critic of both Trump populism and progressive overreach.

name: Thomas Friedman
lens: Three-time Pulitzer Prize-winning NYT foreign affairs columnist. Frames geopolitics through globalization, technology, and energy. Influential voice on Middle East peace, China, and climate economics.

name: Ian Bremmer
lens: Founder of Eurasia Group and GZERO Media. Leading political risk analyst who frames global events through geopolitical risk and the erosion of the global liberal order.

name: Casey Newton
lens: Founder of Platformer and NYT Hard Fork podcast co-host. Leading voice on how social media platforms shape democracy, content moderation, and the tech industry's political power.

name: Kevin Roose
lens: NYT tech columnist and Hard Fork podcast co-host. Covers AI, social media, and the human consequences of technology. Known for his viral Bing AI conversation that raised alarms about AI alignment.

name: Tristan Harris
lens: Co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology and star of The Social Dilemma. Frames tech through the lens of attention manipulation and existential AI risk. Testifies frequently before Congress.

name: Timnit Gebru
lens: AI researcher and founder of the DAIR Institute. Fired from Google for research on large language model bias. Leading critic of AI industry from an ethics, racial justice, and labor perspective.

name: Gary Marcus
lens: NYU professor emeritus and prominent AI skeptic. Challenges hype around large language models with cognitive science arguments. Vocal on AI regulation, safety, and the gap between AI marketing and reality.

name: Rand Paul
lens: Kentucky senator and libertarian-leaning Republican. Ophthalmologist by training. Champions civil liberties, non-interventionist foreign policy, and fiscal restraint. Frequent critic of government spending from both parties.

name: John Kennedy
lens: Louisiana senator known for folksy one-liners and viral committee hearing moments. Frames complex policy through memorable Southern wit. A Republican who bridges populist and establishment styles.

name: John Thune
lens: Senate Majority Leader from South Dakota. Represents the Republican establishment's governing wing. Key voice on agriculture, tech regulation, and infrastructure.

name: Amy Klobuchar
lens: Minnesota senator and leading antitrust advocate. Pragmatic centrist Democrat who champions Big Tech regulation, competition policy, and Midwestern economic issues.

name: Sheldon Whitehouse
lens: Rhode Island senator and relentless crusader against dark money in politics and climate denial. Known for his weekly Senate floor speeches on climate change and Supreme Court ethics reform.

name: Mike Lee
lens: Utah senator and constitutional originalist. Champions limited government, states' rights, and deregulation. Among the Senate's most ideologically consistent conservatives.

name: Pete Ricketts
lens: Nebraska senator and former governor. Business-oriented Republican from the Ameritrade family. Focuses on agriculture, trade, and Midwestern economic interests.

name: Cori Bush
lens: Former Missouri representative and activist from the Ferguson uprising. One of the most progressive voices in Congress on racial justice, defunding police, and Palestine.

name: Maxwell Frost
lens: First Gen Z member of Congress from Florida. Former March for Our Lives organizer. Brings a youth-first perspective on gun violence, climate, and housing affordability.

name: Anna Paulina Luna
lens: Florida representative and Air Force veteran. Rising MAGA star who brings a Latina and military perspective to conservative messaging. Vocal on border security and veterans issues.

name: Thomas Massie
lens: Kentucky representative and MIT-educated engineer. The House's most independent Republican, voting against his own party on spending, surveillance, and foreign aid. Libertarian streak on civil liberties.

name: Pramila Jayapal
lens: Washington state representative and chair of the Congressional Progressive Caucus. Immigrant from India who leads on Medicare for All, immigration reform, and workers' rights.

name: Michael McCaul
lens: Texas representative and House Foreign Affairs Committee chair. Leading Republican voice on China competition, Ukraine support, and national security. Represents the internationalist wing of the GOP.

name: Ritchie Torres
lens: Bronx representative and one of the first openly gay Afro-Latino members of Congress. Centrist Democrat who breaks with progressives on Israel and housing policy. Voice for working-class urban communities.

name: Lauren Boebert
lens: Colorado representative and gun-rights firebrand. Provocative MAGA voice who frames issues through Second Amendment absolutism, anti-government regulation, and culture war battles.

name: Greg Abbott
lens: Texas governor and leading voice on border security. Has bused migrants to Democratic cities and deployed state troops to the border. Key figure in the states' rights versus federal authority debate.

name: Gretchen Whitmer
lens: Michigan governor and rising Democratic star. Pragmatic moderate who won in a swing state. Focuses on manufacturing, infrastructure, and reproductive rights. Potential future presidential contender.

name: Wes Moore
lens: Maryland governor, combat veteran, and bestselling author. First Black governor of Maryland. Frames policy through economic opportunity, service, and bridging partisan divides.

name: RAND Corporation
lens: Nonpartisan research institution providing objective analysis on national security, defense, education, and health. Known for data-driven policy analysis that informs both parties.

name: Heritage Foundation
lens: Leading conservative think tank behind Project 2025. Produces policy research and frameworks on government reform, deregulation, defense, and traditional values that shape Republican governance.

name: Nessa Diosdado
lens: Texas-based Mexican-born progressive creator for Gen-Z for Change. Amplifies immigrant rights, reproductive justice, and LatinX identity from a first-generation political perspective.

name: Tyler Fischer
lens: Comedian and actor who produces satirical political skits. Openly left the Democratic Party and now critiques progressive policies through comedy from a right-leaning perspective.

name: Ed Choi
lens: Asian-American content creator and podcaster covering news, trends, and cultural identity. Co-hosts the 'What in the Shibal' podcast and brings an Asian-American lens to current events.

name: Deja Foxx
lens: Gen Z political activist, digital strategist, and founder of Gen Z Girl Gang. One of the youngest-ever campaign staffers on Kamala Harris's 2020 run. Advocates for abortion rights, voter mobilization, and social justice.

name: Alex Haraus
lens: Environmental activist and content creator who has met with Biden administration climate advisers. Campaigns against oil drilling and gas terminals while promoting sustainable living.

name: Bo Loudon
lens: Young conservative content creator with close ties to the Trump family, particularly Barron Trump. Son of conservative media figures. Produces Gen Z-targeted right-wing commentary.

name: Seth Thomas
lens: Harvard PhD ecology student and National Wildlife Federation fellow. Wildlife biologist who creates evidence-based climate and conservation content from an academic perspective.

name: George Sedberry Jr. (White George)
lens: White Southerner turned anti-racist educator who taught himself Black history while incarcerated. Travels to HBCUs to spotlight their legacy. Confronts his own racist upbringing publicly.

name: John Walsh
lens: Comedian and writer who produced content for the Harris for President campaign. Creates political parodies and skits that satirize Trump and Republican politics.

name: Brent MacLerie
lens: British-born anti-misogyny educator and men's mental health advocate. Creates content promoting healthy masculinity and relationship dynamics, pushing back against far-right gender narratives.
